data.description:
## Status

**Parked as an idea mid-brainstorm (2026-07-18).** The design is NOT final. We paused at
Decision 2 (freshness). "Open decisions" at the end records exactly where to resume. This
ticket is the durable, self-contained capture of the research, discussion, and decisions so
far, per the user's request to "park this as an idea with full details."

## Purpose

Standardize a **mandatory, compact handoff structure** for interrupted or reassigned agent
work. Rebar already has durable tickets, comments, scratch, git state, and claims; what is
missing is a fixed, small contract that lets a fresh context (the same agent after its window
dies, a different agent, or an orchestrator) **reproduce a known-good baseline and understand
the in-flight state** instead of reconstructing assumptions from scratch.

## Two failure modes it kills

1. Treating partially-completed work as a sound foundation **without validating it**.
2. Spending a new context window **reconstructing assumptions that should have been
   externalized**.

It also gives the orchestrator a structured way to distinguish **ordinary continuation** from
a **plan-invalidating event** (baseline no longer reproduces).

The originally-proposed 10 fields: (1) Known-good commit, (2) Baseline verification command,
(3) Baseline result, (4) Current change objective, (5) Files/contracts changed, (6) Unresolved
observations/hypotheses, (7) Currently failing checks, (8) Next bounded action,
(9) Stop/escalation condition, (10) Rollback point.

---

## Research — existing rebar building blocks (reuse vs off-limits)

Verified against source by three exploration passes. Key file:line anchors preserved so a
resumer can go straight to them.

- **`verify_commands`** event/state + `set_verify_commands`/`get_verify_commands` lib API —
  already stores per-ticket verification commands, synced, first-class. This IS field #2
  (baseline verification command). Shape: `{dd_id?, dd_text?, command}`.
- **`commits`** / `attach_commits` (lib) — commit provenance on a ticket. Home for field #1
  (known-good commit / rollback point).
- **`file_impact`** event/state (`{path, reason}`) + `get/set_file_impact` — home for field #5
  (files/contracts changed). Note: it records *intended impact*; the resumer wants the *delta
  since baseline*.
- **`session_log`** ticket type (`docs/event-schema.md:69-100`) — the existing append-only
  **handoff trail** (narrative history). Gate/lifecycle-exempt; cannot be claimed/transitioned;
  searchable but hidden from `list`; non-blocking links only; never synced to Jira. Content
  lives in COMMENTs. Local git-ignored pointer `.rebar/current_session_log` **auto-rotates per
  session fingerprint** (`REBAR_SESSION_ID → CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID → SESSION_ID`); degrade-safe
  when no fingerprint. This is the *history/trail*; the resumption contract is the *current
  distilled state* — complementary (tiered-memory pattern).
- **`scratch`** (`src/rebar/_commands/scratch.py`) — per-ticket key/value string store, BUT
  **filesystem-only, never synced, no lock, purged on ticket close/delete**, 96 KiB/value.
  **OFF-LIMITS as the substrate** — a resumer on another machine would never see it. The
  handoff MUST live in the synced store (events on the `tickets` branch, auto-committed +
  auto-pushed to `sync.remote`).
- **Comments** — freeform `body` string + fixed metadata (`author`, `timestamp`); non-string
  bodies are JSON-coerced to a string. No first-class structured payload — structure must be
  serialized into the body.
- **Claim** — one-shot `open → in_progress`; stamps only scalar session-provenance keys
  (`claimed_session`, `claim_harness`, `claim_remote_session`). **There is NO pause / release /
  yield / un-claim operation, and no `resumed_by`/`resumed_at`.** Transition *into* `in_progress`
  (from `open`, `blocked`, or reactivating `closed`) is gated identically to claim.
- **Gates** — inline, **opt-in, default-OFF**, funneled through one seam
  (`src/rebar/_commands/gates.py`, keyed `verify.<flag>`, fail-open on unreadable config). Two
  today: the **plan-review claim gate** (on claim + transition-to-in_progress) and the
  **completion-verifier close gate** (on `→ closed`, fail-closed). This seam is the natural
  wiring point if "reproduce baseline before continuing" becomes an enforced gate. NB: default-off
  cuts against calling anything "mandatory by default."
- **Description "templates"** — NOT a stored scaffold; enforced as a **clarity-gate scoring
  heuristic** (`_clarity_score`, `gates.py:110-167`) that rewards per-type `##` headings.
- **Naming collision:** "checkpoint" and "baseline" are already taken — by the plan-review LLM
  engine and the reconciler three-way-merge respectively. Pick a distinct name (e.g.
  "resumption", "handoff").

## Research — prior-art synthesis (what to keep, add, avoid)

**The reproducibility trio (#1 commit, #2 command, #3 result) is the design's genuine
differentiator.** Most human and AI handoff formats only *soft drift-check* and assume the
workspace is intact; re-deriving a verified baseline is the strongest, best-established idea in
the body of prior art and this design has it front-and-center. Backing: git bisect
known-good/known-bad + adjudicating test; CI "last-known-good / green trunk"; TDD RED→GREEN
("one small step from green"); SLSA provenance + in-toto ("verify before trust"); reproducible
-builds ("same source + env + instructions"); "Reflections on Trusting Trust" / diverse
double-compiling (don't trust inherited state; independently reproduce).

**Additions prior art demanded (ranked), and our disposition:**

- **Receiver read-back / re-verification step** (I-PASS "Synthesis"; SBARR/ISBARR read-back;
  aviation challenge-response; ICS face-to-face transfer; SLSA VSA digest-match). The single
  biggest structural pattern: the contract as proposed is **write-only**; every mature protocol
  added a receiver step. The user's spec ALREADY includes this ("the resumer first reproduces
  the baseline") — it is the strongest part, so enforcement should bite HERE. → **Decision 4
  (open).**
- **Decisions / dead-ends layer** (Cognition "Don't Build Multi-Agents": compress history into
  *decisions*; Anthropic context-engineering: preserve architectural decisions;
  `claude-handoff` format calls "Failed Approaches" its "most valuable part"; blameless
  postmortem "who decided X and why"). → **Folded into field #6 (open + closed), with a pointer
  to the comment trail** (my recommendation; standing unless vetoed).
- **Freshness stamp** (SHA + timestamp + branch on the record; SLSA identity; SRE
  "last-updated by jennifer"; docs-rot). Kills the "note written at step 3, read at step 30,
  silently lies" mode. → **APPROVED.**
- **Environment / toolchain fingerprint** (reproducible-builds "same commit ≠ same baseline";
  hermetic builds; MCVE env). → **DROPPED** (project builds not env-sensitive enough to earn a
  usually-noise field).
- **Owner / next-owner / escalation target** (ICS, SRE, PagerDuty, OPORD). Partially covered by
  field #9. → **not yet decided.**
- **Definition-of-done / desired end-state / commander's intent — the "why"** (SRE Exit
  Criteria; Agile DoD; OPORD end-state). The fallback when field #8's next-action turns out
  wrong. → **not yet decided (tie to field #4).**
- **Severity / blast-radius**, **in-flight/pending work** (running CI, spawned sub-agents,
  awaited reviews — field #7 captures failing, not pending). → severity not adopted; pending-work
  noted as a #7 refinement.

**Field refinements prior art suggests (not yet locked):** #3 baseline result should be
*evidence* (exit code / digest), state what "green" covers, and have a **third "unverifiable"**
state (git bisect exit 125), not a boolean. #4 should separate *what* (objective) from *why*
(intent/end-state). #7 should carry expected-vs-actual and a deterministic-vs-flaky qualifier.
#8 should be specific, measurable, idempotent/safe-to-replay. #9 should be plural if-then +
named escalation target.

## Research — failure modes to design around

- **Mandatory write-side template = documented failure.** Ontario mandated the WHO surgical
  checklist: 92–100% compliance, **zero** measurable benefit (NEJM 2014) — pure box-ticking.
  Goodhart's / Campbell's law, normalization-of-deviance, and cargo-cult process all predict a
  "template completed" target becomes a plausible fill, not truth. A 20k-session study found
  agents "prematurely claim success" 27.6% of the time; agents emit "all tests pass" even when
  hidden tests fail. → **Put the mandate on the machine-verifiable resume action (re-run the
  baseline), keep the self-authored fields lean and evidence-bearing, grade *resumption
  success*, not fields-filled.**
- **Handoff is written at the worst moment** — interruption coincides with a full window /
  context rot (least-reliable model) and human-factors omission-under-load. → maintain the
  record continuously; derive machine fields from git/test output, not degraded recall.
- **Staleness / docs-rot** — a note read late actively misleads. → freshness stamp; resumer
  diffs stamp vs reality first.
- **Checklist bloat / "lost in the middle"** — 10 fields is at Gawande's 5–9 ceiling; long
  handoffs are partially ignored (U-shaped attention, arXiv 2307.03172). → killer items only;
  front-load the resume point; cut re-derivable/usually-empty fields.
- **Trusting-trust circularity** — never trust inherited "result: green"; the resumer
  independently re-derives.

**Two structural patterns the research repeatedly points to:** (a) **split by direction** —
*writing* = DO-CONFIRM (short verification pause on killer items); *resuming* = READ-DO (an
ordered recipe of commands the resumer executes to re-establish + confirm the baseline before
continuing). (b) **tier memory** — compact contract PLUS a pointer to the full searchable trace
(session_log / comments).

---

## Decision log (locked so far)

1. **Freshness stamp — ADDED.** Record carries its own commit SHA + timestamp + branch.
2. **Rollback point merged into known-good commit** — one field (CI treats last-known-good as
   both baseline and revert target).
3. **Environment fingerprint — DROPPED.**
4. **Decisions/dead-ends — folded into field #6** (reframe as open + closed items, with a
   pointer to the comment trail), not a new 11th field. *My recommendation; standing unless the
   user vetoes.*

## Current field list (evolving)

1. **Known-good commit** (also the rollback point) — reuse `commits`/`attach_commits`.
2. **Baseline verification command** — reuse `verify_commands`.
3. **Baseline result** — evidence (exit code/digest); three states incl. "unverifiable" *(refine)*.
4. **Current change objective** — possibly split what vs why/end-state *(open)*.
5. **Files/contracts changed** — reuse `file_impact`; foreground delta-since-baseline.
6. **Open + closed items** — unresolved observations/hypotheses AND ruled-out approaches /
   settled decisions, with a pointer to the comment trail *(folded, per Decision 4 above)*.
7. **Currently failing checks** — + expected-vs-actual, deterministic-vs-flaky, pending/in-flight *(refine)*.
8. **Next bounded action** — specific, measurable, idempotent/safe-to-replay *(refine)*.
9. **Stop / escalation condition** — plural if-then + named escalation target *(refine)*.
10. **Freshness stamp** — NEW, approved.

## Open decisions (RESUME HERE)

- **Decision 2 — freshness (PAUSED HERE).** Continuous-at-checkpoints *(my lean: robust to
  ungraceful interruption; stamp makes lag visible)* vs write-at-interruption *(simpler, but the
  interruptions you most need to survive are the ungraceful ones)*.
- **Decision 3 — who fills it in.** Hand-typed vs auto-derive the mechanical fields
  (commit / failing checks / changed files) from git/test output, agent adds only judgment
  fields. (Determines whether "continuous" is realistic or ceremony.)
- **Decision 4 — what the resumer MUST do.** Read only vs must re-run the baseline before it is
  allowed to continue (the receiver read-back the prior art most strongly endorses).
- **Decision 5 — what "go into diagnosis" is.** A real recorded task state the orchestrator can
  see, vs behavioral guidance only.
- **Decision 6 — how mandatory, enforced by what.** Rebar code gate (via the `gates.py` seam)
  vs skill/convention. This also decides whether rebar core changes at all, or it's a protocol
  layered on existing primitives (session_log + verify_commands + a description convention).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Brainstorm resumed and Decisions 2–6 settled before any implementation.
- [ ] Final design records purpose, edge-case behavior, and a high-confidence implementation
      plan for the chosen substrate/enforcement.
- [ ] (When built) implementation tickets authored to pass the plan-review gate.
data.title: Session-resumption contract — standardize a compact handoff for interrupted/reassigned work (DESIGN IN PROGRESS)
